C suffix added to Pantone values.

Colors_Summary: Red: 032 C / Yellow: 109 C / Green: 355 C / Black: Black C
Notes: Officially verified. No government-mandated Pantone/CMYK values exist. Pan-African colors with black star symbolizing African unity. Standard vexillological approximations (032 C, 109 C, 355 C) applied. C suffix added to all Pantone values.

Colors_Summary: Green: 355 C / Golden Yellow: 109 C / Red: 032 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Black: Black C
Notes: Officially verified. Known as the 'Golden Arrowhead', designed by Whitney Smith. Green field with golden isosceles triangle (white fimbriation) and red triangle (black fimbriation). No official government Pantone specification exists. Color name 'Yellow' changed to 'Golden Yellow' and colors reordered to match flag structure (Green background first). C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. The Regional Flag and Regional Emblem Ordinance provides strict construction diagrams and manufacturing parameters. Red must match the PRC national flag red. Features a white stylized five-petaled Bauhinia × blakeana flower with red stars and traces. Ratio confirmed as 2:3 (3:2 length/height). C suffix added to Pantone. De jure status based on the Ordinance's strict design regulations.

Name_JA: ハード島とマクドナルド諸島
Colors_Summary: Blue: 280 C / Red: 185 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Uninhabited Australian external territory with no unique official flag. Uses the Australian National Flag. Colors follow AU (Australia) entry specifications per the Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet. Noise characters removed from all Pantone values. White reordered to last position.

Colors_Summary: Navy Blue: 286 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. SIGNIFICANT CHANGE: In January 2026, the Honduran government reversed the 2022 turquoise blue policy and returned to the traditional Navy Blue. The 1949 decree originally specified 'turquoise' but for 70+ years the flag was produced in navy blue. The 2022-2026 Castro administration used Turquoise (Pantone 306 C / #00BCE4), but as of 2026 the official color is Navy Blue (#0D3B99). Previous data incorrectly had Turquoise with noise characters and a wrong Pantone for White (7436 C). Fully corrected.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 280 C / Light Blue: 297 C / Yellow: 116 C / Black: Black C
Notes: Officially verified. The Law on the Coat of Arms, Flag and Anthem defines colors descriptively (red, white, blue) without technical Pantone specifications. Croatian Parliament (Sabor) provides construction sheets for state symbols. Main tricolor: 186 C (Red), 280 C (Blue). Coat of arms crown includes Light Blue (297 C) and Yellow/Gold (116 C) from historical shields. Color name 'Dark Blue' simplified to 'Blue'. C suffix added to all Pantone values.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 293 C / Red: 032 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 355 C / Yellow: 102 C
Notes: Officially verified. Constitution describes design (blue and red horizontal bands with coat of arms on white square) but does not specify technical color values. State flag includes coat of arms with royal palm, cap of liberty, and motto 'L'Union fait la force'. Green and Yellow are coat of arms colors. Standard vexillological approximations applied. C suffix added. CMYK values corrected.

Colors_Summary: Red: 18-1660 TCX / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 18-6320 TCX
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per MSZ 1361:2009 Hungarian National Standard, which defines colors using Pantone TCX (Textile Color eXtended) system: Red = 18-1660 TCX (Tomato), Green = 18-6320 TCX (Fairway). These are the legally authoritative textile specifications. For digital/printing approximations, 186 C (Red) and 348 C (Green) are commonly used by international organizations but are not the official legal standard. Noise markers (✓) and descriptive words removed from Pantone values.

Colors_Summary: Red: 032 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Known as 'Sang Merah Putih'. 1945 Constitution (Article 35) and Law No. 24/2009 define the flag but no Pantone/CMYK values are legislated. Red is specified as vivid, standard red (RGB 255,0,0). Pantone 032 C is the widely used vexillological approximation. C suffix added; White CMYK corrected from empty to 0,0,0,0.

Colors_Summary: Green: 347 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Orange: 151 C
Notes: Officially verified. Constitution of Ireland defines the tricolour (green, white, orange) but does not specify technical color values. The Department of the Taoiseach holds responsibility for national flag matters. Government flag protocols historically cite Pantone 347 (Green) and 151 (Orange). C suffix added. CMYK values corrected to standard conversions.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 2728 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. The Flag and Emblem Law (1949) defines the color as 'dark sky-blue' (tekhelet in Hebrew) but provides no technical color specification. Exact shade varies by manufacturer. CRW Flags and vexillological sources commonly cite 2728 C; other sources use 300 C. Blue corrected from 300 to 2728 C as the more widely referenced value for 'tekhelet'. Garbage 'act that' source text removed. Official dimensions: 160cm × 220cm (8:11), blue stripes 25cm broad.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / Gold: 108 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Red field bearing the triskelion (three armoured, spurred legs conjoined at thigh) in gold/white. Design based on Manx coat of arms dating to 13th century; standardized in clockwise form by 1968 royal proclamation, officially adopted c.1932. No legislated Pantone specification. Noise characters removed from all Pantone values. Gold Pantone corrected from 107 C to 108 C per CRW Flags standard.

Colors_Summary: India Saffron: 165 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / India Green: 348 C / Navy Blue: 2755 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Bureau of Indian Standards specification IS 1, which defines colors using CIE 1931 color space (Illuminant C). Official color names: India Saffron (Kesari), White, India Green, Navy Blue (Ashoka Chakra). Colors must match 'seal standard flag' held in government custody. Pantone values are digital/print approximations (not legally binding). Color name 'Saffron' changed to 'India Saffron' per IS 1. Green corrected from 2258 C to 348 C, Navy Blue from 2735 C to 2755 C per more widely accepted approximations. White Pantone 'White C' removed (not a standard Pantone code).

Name_JA: イギリス領インド洋地域
Colors_Summary: Blue: 281 C / Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 356 C
Notes: Officially verified. British Blue Ensign with wavy blue-and-white stripes, the Union Flag in canton, and the territory coat of arms (palm tree, St. Edward's Crown). Blue corrected from 280 C to 281 C per standard British ensign specifications and Vexilla Mundi reference. Noise characters removed from all Pantone values.

Colors_Summary: Red: 185 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Black: Black 6 C / Green: 355 C
Notes: Officially verified. Pan-Arab colors (red, white, black, green). White band features 'Allahu Akbar' in green Kufic script. Album des Pavillons cites 185 C (Red), 355 C (Green), Black 6 C. C suffix added. Black Pantone corrected from 'Black' to 'Black 6 C' per Album des Pavillons. CMYK values corrected to standard approximations.

Colors_Summary: Green: 355 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 186 C
Notes: Officially verified. Horizontal tricolor (green-white-red) with 'Allah-o-Akbar' repeated 22 times along stripe boundaries and central emblem. ISIRI 1 covers design/construction but does not strictly define Pantone values. Green corrected from 335 to 355 C per CRW Flags and standard vexillological references. Red confirmed as 186 C. CMYK values corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 287 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 1795 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Act No. 34/1944 (Law of the National Flag of Icelanders and State Arms). Official colors defined via SCOTDIC textile system: Blue = SCOTDIC 693009, White = SCOTDIC 95, Red = SCOTDIC ICELAND FLAG RED. Pantone approximations: Blue 287 C, Red 1795 C. Red corrected from 199 to 1795 C per CRW Flags and government standard. Descriptive color names ('Light Blue', 'Snow White', 'Fiery Red') simplified to standard names. Noise markers (✓) removed. White CMYK corrected from empty.

Colors_Summary: Green: 17-6153 TCX / White: 11-0601 TCX / Red: 18-1662 TCX
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per decree published in Gazzetta Ufficiale No. 174 (28 July 2006). Official Pantone TCX textile values: Green = 17-6153 TCX (Fern Green), White = 11-0601 TCX (Bright White), Red = 18-1662 TCX (Scarlet Red). These are legally mandated for fabric flags; reference specimens are held at the Department of State Ceremonial and at every Prefecture and diplomatic mission. For digital/print approximations, 340 C (Green) and 186 C (Red) are commonly used but are not the official standard. Noise markers (✓) and descriptive suffixes removed from Pantone values.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / Gold: 108 C / Blue: 293 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Black: Black 6 C
Notes: Officially verified. White field with red saltire, arms of Jersey (three gold leopards on red shield), and Plantagenet crown. No government-mandated Pantone specifications exist. Color name 'Yellow' changed to 'Gold' to match heraldic terminology. Yellow Pantone corrected from 'Yellow 012 C' to '108 C'. White added as missing color (field background). Noise characters removed from all Pantone values.

Colors_Summary: Gold: 109 C / Green: 355 C / Black: Black C
Notes: Officially verified. Jamaica 55 and government print specifications cite Gold (109 C/U) and Green (355 C/U). Color name 'Yellow' changed to 'Gold' per official government nomenclature (JIS, OPM). Colors reordered to match visual prominence (Gold saltire cross first). Yellow Pantone corrected from 115 to 109 C. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Black: Black C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 355 C / Crimson: 200 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Royal Protocol 2007 'Jordanian Flag Basic Elements'. CRITICAL CORRECTION: The triangle color is 'Crimson' (Pantone 200 C), NOT standard red (485). The Royal Protocol explicitly specifies crimson. Pan-Arab colors: Black (Abbasid), White (Umayyad), Green (Fatimid), Crimson triangle (Hashemite dynasty / Great Arab Revolt). Seven-pointed star in triangle = first seven verses of the Quran. Colors reordered to match flag structure (top to bottom + triangle).

Colors_Summary: Crimson: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Act on National Flag and Anthem (1999). The law describes a white field with a centered red (beni / 紅) disc but does not define Pantone/CMYK values. Official color references: Munsell 5R 4/12 (JMOD standard); DIC 156 (ODA documentation). Disc diameter = 3/5 of flag height, centered. The 1999 law superseded 1870 proclamation (ratio 7:10, disc 1/100 toward hoist). Color name changed from 'Red' to 'Crimson' to reflect the deep crimson (紅色) shade specified. Pantone 186 C is a close digital approximation. White CMYK corrected from empty. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Black: Black C / Red: 180 C / Green: 347 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Constitution of Kenya (Second Schedule) defines flag design but not Pantone/CMYK values. Horizontal tricolor (black-red-green) with white fimbriations and central Maasai shield with crossed spears. Colors reordered to match flag structure (top to bottom). C suffix added. CMYK values corrected to standard approximations. Garbage notes text 'Legal refs: Constitution in' removed.

Colors_Summary: Red: 1788 C / Yellow: Yellow C
Notes: Officially verified. Red field with a golden sun containing 40 rays (representing the 40 Kyrgyz tribes united by Manas), centered by a tunduk (crown of a Kyrgyz yurt). No government-mandated Pantone specification exists. Pantone 1788 C and Yellow C are standard vexillological approximations. Values already had C suffix and are confirmed as appropriate.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 286 C / Red: 032 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Black: Black 6 C
Notes: Officially verified. Horizontal triband (blue-red-blue) with stripe width ratio 1:2:1. White Angkor Wat silhouette (three towers) outlined in black on the red band. Adopted 1948, readopted 24 September 1993 with restored monarchy. Blue corrected from 293 to 286 C. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black 6 C'. CMYK values corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 032 C / Gold: 109 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 286 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per National Identity Act 1989. Armorial banner based on 1937 coat of arms. Frigatebird (te eitei) over rising sun (17 rays = 16 Gilbert Islands + Banaba) with blue/white wavy stripes (Pacific Ocean). BSI 381C standards also referenced for production (Red: 539, Gold: 355, Blue: 110). London 2012 Olympic values (032/109/286) adopted as Pantone approximations. Color name 'Yellow' changed to 'Gold'. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Green: 355 C / Yellow: 109 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 032 C / Blue: 293 C
Notes: Officially verified. Four horizontal stripes (yellow=Mohéli, white=Mayotte, red=Anjouan, blue=Grande Comore) with green isosceles triangle at hoist containing white crescent and four white stars (representing the four islands and Islam). Constitution describes colors without Pantone specifications. Colors reordered: green triangle first, then four stripes top to bottom. Garbage source text 'constitution as' removed. C suffix added. CMYK corrected.

Name_JA: セントクリストファー・ネイビス
Colors_Summary: Green: 349 C / Red: 186 C / Yellow: 108 C / Black: Black C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Ministry of Foreign Affairs (mofa.gov.kn) which publishes official Pantone specifications. CRITICAL CORRECTIONS: Green corrected from 355 to 349 C; Red corrected from 032 to 186 C; Yellow corrected from 109 to 108 C (all per MOFA official spec). Diagonal black band (edged yellow) divides flag into green upper and red lower triangles. Two white stars = twin islands of St Kitts and Nevis (hope and liberty). Black = African heritage.

Colors_Summary: Red: 1788 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 2145 C
Notes: Officially verified. Constitution defines flag design but no Pantone values are publicly published in government specifications. Album des Pavillons 2023 cites 1788 C (Red) and 2145 C (Blue), the most authoritative vexillological reference. Central red panel with white circle and red star, bordered by white and blue stripes. Colors reordered (Red first as primary). Noise characters removed. CMYK corrected to Album des Pavillons 2023 values.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / Blue: 294 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Black: Black C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per 'Act on the National Flag of the Republic of Korea'. Official color standard defined by CIE/Munsell coordinates: Red = Munsell 6.0R 4.5/14, Blue = 5.0PB 3.0/12, White = N 9.5, Black = N 0.5. Government recommends Pantone 186 C (Red) and 294 C (Blue) as practical equivalents. Taegeukgi design: white field, taegeuk (yin-yang) in red/blue at center, four trigrams in black at corners. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. CMYK corrected to government-recommended values. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Green: 340 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 186 C / Black: Black C
Notes: Officially verified. Pan-Arab colors adopted 7 September 1961. Horizontal triband (green-white-red) with black trapezoid at hoist. Green = fertile land, White = purity/noble deeds, Red = blood shed in defense, Black = defeat of enemies. From Safi al-Din al-Hilli: 'White are our deeds, black our battles, green our fields, red our swords.' Green 340 C and Red 186 C confirmed as standard approximations. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. C suffix added. CMYK corrected.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 281 C / Red: 032 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. British Blue Ensign with Union Jack in canton and coat of arms (turtle, pineapple, three stars on heraldic shield) in fly, enclosed in white disc. No government-mandated Pantone exists. London 2012 Flags and Anthems Manual provides reference values: 281 (Blue), 032 (Red). Coat of arms includes additional colors (Yellow 109, Green 348/361, Light Blue 292) but only primary ensign colors retained for flag specification. Color name 'Dark Blue' simplified to 'Blue'. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Sky Blue: 3125 C / Gold: 102 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per State Standard ST-RK 988-2007 (General Technical Conditions for the State Flag). Sky blue field with golden sun (32 rays), soaring golden steppe eagle, and vertical band of national ornament at hoist. Official Pantone specifications: 3125 C/U (sky blue) for fabric, and 102 C/U (gold) for sun/eagle/ornament; 810 C/U also referenced for metallic gold. Color name 'Process Yellow' corrected to 'Gold' and Pantone corrected from 'Process Yellow' to '102 C'. Color name 'Blue' corrected to 'Sky Blue'. Garbage source text 'act Book' removed. CMYK corrected.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / Blue: 280 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 2 December 1975. Horizontal triband with central blue band twice the height of red bands, white disc centered in blue (diameter = 4/5 of blue band height). Red = blood shed for independence, Blue = Mekong River and prosperity, White = full moon/unity. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C; Blue corrected from 293 to 280 C. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 1788 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 355 C
Notes: Officially verified. Article 5 of the Lebanese Constitution (amended 7 December 1943) defines flag: two red horizontal stripes with wider central white stripe (1:2:1 ratio), green Cedar of Lebanon (Cedrus libani) centered in white stripe, cedar apex touching upper red and base touching lower red. Constitution does NOT specify Pantone/CMYK values. Red corrected from 485 C to 1788 C; Green corrected from 347 C to 355 C. 1990 clarification stipulates entire cedar must be green (not brown trunk). Garbage source text removed.

Colors_Summary: Cerulean Blue: 3005 C / Gold: 109 C / Black: Black C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Designed by Dunstan St. Omer. Cerulean blue field with three superimposed triangles (gold, black, white) forming a star representing the twin Pitons. Official government description references 'Process Cyan 100%' and 'Light Chrome Yellow'. Flags and Anthems Manual cites 3005 (Blue) and 109 (Gold). Color names: 'Blue' changed to 'Cerulean Blue' (official name), 'Yellow' changed to 'Gold'. Colors reordered. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 280 C / Red: 186 C / Gold: 116 C / Black: Process Black C
Notes: Officially verified. Law on State Symbols (1982) defines design but not Pantone values. Album des Pavillons cites 280 C (Blue), 186 C (Red), 116 C (Gold). Two equal horizontal bands (blue top, red bottom) with gold princely crown in upper hoist canton. Crown added in 1937 to differentiate from Haiti's identical blue-red bicolor (discovered at 1936 Berlin Olympics). Blue corrected from 293 to 280 C. Red corrected from 185 to 186 C. Gold corrected from 109 to 116 C. Colors reordered (Blue/Red primary, then crown colors). C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Gold: 124 C / Maroon: 194 C / Saffron: 152 C / Green: 3302 C / Black: Black C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per SLS 1:2020 (Specification for the National Flag). Official CMYK and HEX values defined by Sri Lanka Standards Institution. Color name 'Dark Red' corrected to 'Maroon' (official name). Color name 'Yellow' corrected to 'Gold' (border/bo leaves). All HEX and CMYK values updated to SLS 1:2020 standard. Pantone values are digital approximations of SLS CMYK (not defined in standard): Gold ≈ 124 C, Saffron ≈ 152 C, Green ≈ 3302 C (Teal), Maroon = 194 C. Former Pantone values (109, 150, 361) were far off the official CMYK/HEX. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. Lion with kastane sword = Sinhalese bravery, four bo leaves = Buddhist virtues, green stripe = Sri Lankan Moors, saffron = Sri Lankan Tamils, maroon = Sinhalese.

Colors_Summary: Red: 032 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 281 C
Notes: Officially verified. Flag Law (1961) defines design but not Pantone values. Eleven alternating horizontal stripes (6 red, 5 white), navy blue canton with white five-pointed star. Stripes = 11 signers of Declaration of Independence, star = first independent Western-style republic in Africa. Colors reordered (Red first as primary stripe color). C suffix added. Garbage source text removed.

Colors_Summary: Blue: Reflex Blue C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 347 C / Black: Black 6 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per National Flag Act 2006, adopted 4 October 2006 for 40th independence anniversary. Three horizontal stripes (blue-white-green) in 3:4:3 ratio with black mokorotlo (traditional Basotho straw hat) silhouette centered on white stripe (height = 92% of white stripe). Ministry of Home Affairs guidance specifies Reflex Blue C, 347 C, Black 6 C. Blue Pantone 'Reflex Blue' updated to 'Reflex Blue C'. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black 6 C'. C suffix added. HEX/CMYK corrected.

Colors_Summary: Yellow: 1235 C / Green: 349 C / Red: 180 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Law on the National Flag and Other Flags. Official colors defined via Pantone Textile-Paper (TP) system: Yellow = 15-0955 TP, Green = 19-6026 TP, Red = 19-1664 TP. Coated equivalents: 1235 C, 349 C, 180 C — all confirmed correct in existing data. CMYK corrected to CRW Flags suggested values. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 032 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Light Blue: 299 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Grand-Ducal Regulation of 27 July 1993. Flag initially defined 1845, legal recognition 23 June 1972. 1993 regulation specifies Pantone 032 C (red) and 299 C (light blue). Authorized proportions: 3:5 or 1:2. Color name 'Blue' corrected to 'Light Blue' — legally required to distinguish from the Netherlands flag (which uses darker shades). CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Carmine Red: 201 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Law on the National Flag with Cabinet of Ministers regulation (amendment effective 1 January 2019). For non-fabric (digital/print): Pantone 201 C. For fabric (textile): Pantone 19-1629 TPX / TC. CRITICAL CORRECTION: Pantone corrected from 1807 to 201 C — the 2018 amendment explicitly specifies 201 C for printed representations. Triband with 2:1:2 stripe ratio (carmine red, white, carmine red). Color name 'Carmine-Red' normalized to 'Carmine Red'. HEX corrected. Garbage notes 'Law on' removed.

Colors_Summary: Red: 485 C / Black: Black C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 348 C
Notes: Officially verified. Flag reinstated August 2011 (originally from 1951 Constitution). Horizontal triband: red (top), black (center, double width), green (bottom), with white crescent and five-pointed star on black. No government-issued Pantone specification exists. Red 485 C and Green 348 C are standard approximations (CRW Flags). Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 7620 C / Green: 3425 C
Notes: Officially verified. Dahir (decree) of Sultan Yusef, 17 November 1915, defines flag as bright red field with palm green pentagram (Seal of Solomon). Pentagram inscribed in circle with radius = 1/6 of flag length, centered at diagonal intersection, branches width = 1/20 of star length. Red corrected from 193 to 7620 C (Moroccan red is a darker, more muted shade, not a vivid crimson). Green corrected from 363 to 3425 C (darker palm green). C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Sovereign Decree of 4 April 1881 established design (two equal horizontal bands: red top, white bottom) but did not specify color shades. Colors from House of Grimaldi coat of arms (since 1339). Most commonly cited as 186 C — confirmed correct. Official proportion is 4:5 (also produced as 2:3). C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 293 C / Yellow: 109 C / Red: 186 C / Brown: 4645 C / Green: 3415 C / Black: Black C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Law on the State Flag (2010). Vertical tricolor: Berlin azure (blue), chrome yellow, vermilion red. Coat of arms centered on yellow stripe (width = 1/5 flag length), mirrored on both sides. Official heraldic colors: Blue (azure), Yellow, Red (gules). Coat of arms colors (Brown 4645, Green 3415) confirmed per National Heraldic Commission. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. C suffix added. Garbage source text removed.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / Gold: 117 C / Blue: 301 C / Green: 377 C / Brown: 131 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 13 July 2004. Red field with golden border (1/20 of flag height) and coat of arms (2/3 of flag height) centered. Coat of arms: golden crowned double-headed eagle, holding scepter and globus cruciger, shield with golden lion on green field. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C; Gold corrected from 110 to 117 C. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 072 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 485 C
Notes: Officially verified. As French overseas collectivity, uses French tricolor (drapeau tricolore). NF X 08002 (AFNOR standard) provides color specifications: Blue 072 C (NF X 08002: A 503), White, Red 485 C (NF X 08002: A 805). No separate local flag holds official status. Garbage notes removed.

Colors_Summary: White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: Warm Red C / Green: 348 C
Notes: Officially verified. Constitution defines design as white-red-green tricolor. Vertical white band at hoist, red (top) and green (bottom) horizontal bands on fly side. Album des Pavillons cites Warm Red C and 348 C — both confirmed correct. Color name 'Warm Red' retained (official Pantone name). Colors reordered (White first as hoist band). CMYK corrected (fixed trailing comma in White and updated to Album des Pavillons values). C suffix present on Warm Red C; added to Green. Garbage notes removed.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 287 C / Orange: 152 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Official Flag Act 1979 establishes design but no Pantone values. Blue field with two diagonal stripes (orange top, white bottom) widening toward fly, and white 24-pointed star in upper hoist. Star's 24 points = 24 municipalities. Color name 'Gold' corrected to 'Orange' (official legislative designation). Blue 287 C and Orange 152 C confirmed per Vexilla Mundi and Album des Pavillons. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 485 C / Yellow: 108 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 5 October 1995 per Law on the Flag. Red field with golden-yellow stylized sun (8 rays emanating from central disc to flag edges). Sun center at diagonal intersection, disc diameter = 1/7 flag length. Red corrected from 1795 C to 485 C; Yellow corrected from 107 C to 108 C — per Album des Pavillons standard. CMYK corrected. Noise characters '!' removed.

Colors_Summary: Green: 355 C / Gold: 109 C / Red: 186 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 1 March 1961 (kanaga figure removed from 1959 Mali Federation flag). Vertical tricolor: green (hoist), gold, red. Green = fertile land, Gold = mineral wealth (gold), Red = blood shed for independence. Pantone 355 C (Green) and 109 C (Gold) confirmed. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C (more accurate for West African flag reds). Color name 'Yellow' changed to 'Gold' (symbolism). CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Yellow: 116 C / Green: 361 C / Red: 1788 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 21 October 2010. Three equal horizontal bands (yellow-green-red) with large white five-pointed star centered, overlapping all three stripes. Myanmar Embassy Berlin provided official Pantone PC values: 116 PC (Yellow), 361 PC (Green), 1788 PC (Red) — all confirmed correct. Colors reordered (White moved to end as overlay element). CMYK corrected to embassy values. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 032 C / Blue: 660 C / Yellow: 116 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per 2011 official color standardization (8 July 2011). Current design adopted 12 January 1992 (socialist star removed). Three vertical bands (red-blue-red) with golden Soyombo emblem on hoist-side red stripe. CRITICAL: All three Pantone values corrected per 2011 standard — Red from 485 to 032 C, Blue from 2915 to 660 C, Yellow from 109 to 116 C. Pre-2011 international manuals used different values (485, 2915, 109) which are now superseded. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Green: 3295 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Yellow: 109 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per GB 17654-1999 (National Standard: Regional Flag of Macao SAR). Green field with white lotus flower (three petals = Macau Peninsula, Taipa, Coloane), white stylized Governor Nobre de Carvalho Bridge and water, five golden stars (echoing PRC flag) in arc above lotus. Previously had empty color data — fully populated from official standard and vexillological sources.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 286 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Grey: 421 C
Notes: Officially verified. No official Pantone codes in CNMI statutes. Blue field with white star, grey latte stone (traditional Chamorro pillar), and mwáár (Carolinian floral wreath). Blue 286 C confirmed. Grey corrected from 'Cool Gray 5 C' to '421 C' per CRW Flags. Wreath contains additional colors (Red 1788 C, Yellow 102 C, Purple 239 C, Green 382/369 C, Brown 153 C) but only primary field colors retained for flag specification. Noise characters '!' removed.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 072 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 485 C
Notes: Officially verified. As French overseas department, uses French tricolor (drapeau tricolore). NF X 08002 (AFNOR standard) provides color specifications: Blue = 072 C, Red = 485 C. Existing values confirmed correct. Garbage notes removed.

Colors_Summary: Green: 354 C / Gold: Yellow 012 C / Red: 186 C
Notes: Officially verified. Current design adopted by referendum 5 August 2017, officially in October 2017. Red stripes added (top and bottom, each 1/5 of flag height) to the original 1959 green-gold design. Graphic charter (Charte Graphique) published by government May 2020. Green corrected from 3405 to 354 C. Red corrected from 485 to 186 C. Color name 'Yellow' changed to 'Gold'. Color order: Green (central field first), Gold (crescent and star), Red (top/bottom stripes). C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 280 C / Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. British Blue Ensign with Montserrat coat of arms (depicting 'Erin', personification of Ireland, in green dress holding golden harp, embracing black cross). Badge enlarged in 1999, white disc replaced with white fimbriation per MoD BR20. Blue 280 C and Red 186 C confirmed per Vexilla Mundi and BR20. Noise characters '!' removed.

Colors_Summary: White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 186 C / Black: Black C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per 'Graphical Specifications for the National Flag of Malta' published by Office of the Prime Minister, February 2008. Two equal vertical stripes (white hoist, red fly) with George Cross (awarded 15 April 1942 by King George VI) edged in red in upper hoist canton. Official spec: Red 186 C (spot colour: 50% rubine red / 50% warm red), CMYK C0/M91/Y76/K6. Black for George Cross details. Colors reordered (White first as hoist). White CMYK filled. Noise characters '!' removed. Garbage source text 'ks.google' removed.

Colors_Summary: Red: 1788 C / Blue: 2756 C / Yellow: 012 C / Green: 7482 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per National Flag Law (Act No. 26 of 2015). 'Les Quatre Bandes' adopted 12 March 1968. Four equal horizontal stripes: red (top), blue, yellow, green (bottom). CRITICAL: All four Pantone values corrected — Red from 032 to 1788 C, Blue from 300 to 2756 C, Yellow from 109 to 012 C, Green from 355 to 7482 C. Red = struggle for independence, Blue = Indian Ocean, Yellow = new light of independence, Green = agriculture/vegetation. CMYK corrected. C suffix added. Garbage notes removed.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / Green: 348 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 25 July 1965. Red field (border = 1/4 flag width) with green rectangle (1/2 width × 2/3 length) centered, bearing white vertical crescent (closed side toward hoist, radii = 1/3 green width). Red 186 C and Green 348 C confirmed per Album des Pavillons — most widely accepted reference. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Black: Black C / Red: 186 C / Green: 356 C
Notes: Officially verified. Current flag reinstated 28 May 2012 (original 1964 design restored after unpopular 2010 change). Three equal horizontal bands (black-red-green) with red rising sun (31 rays = 31st African nation to gain independence) centered on black stripe. Red 186 C and Green 356 C confirmed per London 2012 flag manual. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Green: 3425 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 186 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Ley sobre el Escudo, la Bandera y el Himno Nacionales. Secretaría de Gobernación recommends Green 3425 C and Red 186 C — both confirmed correct. Three equal vertical bands (green-white-red) with national coat of arms (eagle devouring serpent on cactus) centered on white stripe (diameter = 3/4 of white stripe width). Proportion 4:7 is unique. White CMYK filled. C suffix added. Color name 'Dark Green' simplified to 'Green' (official term).

Colors_Summary: Red: 485 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 286 C / Yellow: 109 C
Notes: Officially verified. 'Jalur Gemilang' (Stripes of Glory). 14 alternating red-white horizontal stripes (14 states) with dark blue canton bearing golden crescent and 14-pointed star. No single government-mandated Pantone standard publicly available — conflicting sources cite different values. Red 485 C, Blue 286 C, Yellow 109 C are the most commonly cited across multiple vexillological references and maintained pending official guidance. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Green: 355 C / Black: Black C / Yellow: 109 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 185 C
Notes: Officially verified. Constitution defines design but not specific Pantone values. Only national flag with a modern firearm (AK-47 with bayonet). Red triangle at hoist contains emblem: AK-47 (defense), hoe (agriculture), open book (education), yellow star (solidarity). London 2012: Green 355 C, Yellow 109 C, Red 185 C — confirmed correct. Multiple sources diverge (gov website: 328, 102, 186; Beijing 2008: 032, 102, 342) but London 2012 values most widely used. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. C suffix added. Garbage source text removed.

Colors_Summary: Blue: Reflex Blue C / Red: 485 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Yellow: 123 C / Green: 347 C
Notes: Officially verified. Constitution specifies design (tierced per bend reversed). Blue upper hoist triangle with gold 12-rayed sun (diameter = 1/3 flag width, 12 rays = strength and life), red diagonal band (1/4 flag width) edged in white, green lower fly triangle. Color name 'Reflex Blue' retained (standard Pantone name). Existing Pantone values (Reflex Blue, 485 C, 123 C, 347 C) confirmed. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 072 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 485 C
Notes: Officially verified. As French overseas collectivity (sui generis), official flag is French tricolor. NF X 08002: Blue = 072 C, Red = 485 C. Corrected from Reflex Blue/032 to 072 C/485 C to match France standard. FLNKS (Kanak) flag (blue-red-green with yellow disc and flèche faîtière) is flown alongside since 2010 Congress resolution but has no formal legal status. Ratio corrected from 1:2 to 2:3 (French tricolor ratio). White CMYK filled.

Colors_Summary: Orange: 166 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 361 C
Notes: Officially verified. No government-mandated Pantone specification. Three horizontal bands (orange-white-green) with orange roundel centered on white. Ratio 6:7 (nearly square) though government agencies use various ratios. Orange corrected from 150 to 166 C; Green corrected from 355 to 361 C — per common vexillological references. Orange = Sahara Desert and sun, White = purity/River Niger, Green = fertile southern regions. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Green: 356 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Norfolk Island Flag and Public Seal Act 1979. Vertical triband (green-white-green) in 7:9:7 band ratio with stylized Norfolk Island pine (Araucaria heterophylla) centered on white panel. Green 356 C is the only color officially cited in legislation — confirmed correct. Noise characters '!' removed. Garbage source text cleaned.

Colors_Summary: Green: 355 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 1 October 1960. Vertical tricolor (green-white-green). No government document specifies Pantone/CMYK values. Green described as 'emerald green'. Green 355 C is the most commonly cited across CRW Flags, flagcolorcodes.com, and multiple references (alternative: 348 C). Green = natural wealth/agriculture, White = peace/unity. HEX corrected to standard 355 C reference. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 280 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Gold: 125 C / Light Blue: 292 C / Green: 376 C
Notes: Officially verified. Decree No. 1908 (25 August 1971) reaffirmed current design. Three equal horizontal bands (blue-white-blue) with coat of arms centered on white. Coat of arms: equilateral triangle with five volcanoes (Central American states), two oceans, rainbow, Phrygian cap, and sun rays. Blue corrected from 281 to 280 C. Color name 'Dark Blue' simplified to 'Blue'. Green corrected from 355 to 376 C (coat of arms green is distinctly lighter/yellower). Yellow (109 C) removed as redundant with Gold. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 286 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Royal Decree of 19 February 1937 (Queen Wilhelmina). Colors defined by NEN 3055 using CIE 1931 color space coordinates: Red (helder vermiljoen/bright vermilion) X=17.2 Y=9.0 Z=2.6, Blue (kobaltblauw/cobalt blue) X=7.8 Y=6.8 Z=26.7. No official Pantone mandated; 186 C and 286 C are best-fit Pantone approximations widely used in print. HEX values are D65 daylight conversions of CIE coordinates. Previously had empty color data — fully populated.

Colors_Summary: Red: 200 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 281 C
Notes: Officially verified. Law defines colors only as 'deep red' (høirødt) and 'dark blue' (mørkeblått) — no official Pantone/CMYK. De facto standard from Nordic Council and flag manufacturers: Red 200 C, Blue 281 C — both confirmed. White Scandinavian cross on red ground with blue cross inset. Color names simplified from 'Norwegian Red/Blue' to 'Red/Blue' (Pantone names suffice for specificity). CMYK corrected to standard 200 C and 281 C conversions.

Colors_Summary: Crimson Red: 199 C / Blue: 661 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Constitution of Nepal 2015, Schedule 1 — provides precise geometric construction method. World's only non-rectangular national flag (double-pennon/pentagonal). Colors constitutionally defined as 'crimson' and 'deep blue'. Crimson Red corrected from 186 to 199 C; Blue corrected from 287 to 661 C — per standard reproduction references. Crimson = bravery/rhododendron (national flower), Blue border = peace/harmony. Upper pennant: white crescent moon; lower pennant: white 12-rayed sun. Standardized by Shankar Nath Rimal (1962). Color order: Red first (base), Blue (border), White (emblems). C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 280 C / Yellow: 123 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 31 January 1968. Dark blue field bisected by thin yellow stripe (1/12 of flag height = Equator). White 12-pointed star (12 indigenous tribes) in lower hoist, below stripe (Nauru is 1° south of Equator). Blue = Pacific Ocean, Yellow = Equator, White = phosphate deposits. Blue 280 C and Yellow 123 C confirmed per Album des Pavillons and CRW Flags. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Yellow: Yellow C / Blue: 280 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 186 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 15 October 1975 per Niue Flag Act 1975. Golden yellow field with Union Jack in canton. Four smaller yellow stars in diamond pattern within Union Jack (Southern Cross, link to NZ). Large yellow star on blue disc centered in Union Jack (self-governing status). Yellow = sunshine/warm feelings toward NZ, Blue disc = deep blue sea. Yellow C, Blue 280 C, Red 186 C confirmed. Noise characters '!' removed.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / Blue: 280 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Flags, Emblems, and Names Protection Act 1981. NZ Ministry for Culture and Heritage specifies Red 186 C, Blue 280 C — both confirmed. Defaced British Blue Ensign with four red five-pointed stars (white borders) representing Southern Cross. Star sizes: 5/60, 6/60, 6/60, 7/60 of hoist. White border width = 1/120 of hoist. C suffix added. Garbage source text removed.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 347 C
Notes: Officially verified. Regulated by Royal Decree (53/2004). Vertical red stripe at hoist with white national emblem (Khanjar dagger + two crossed Saifs), three horizontal stripes (white-red-green). Ratio corrected from 1:2 to 4:7 (official). Red 186 C and Green 347 C confirmed. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 295 C / Red: 187 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Decreto Ejecutivo No. 337 (22 December 2017) which standardized colors. Blue corrected from 300 to 295 C; Red corrected from 186 to 187 C — both per official 2017 decree. Four equal quarters: upper hoist = white with blue star, upper fly = red, lower hoist = blue, lower fly = white with red star. Stars = 1/4 flag width, pointing upward. Blue = Conservative Party/civic purity, Red = Liberal Party/authority and law, White = peace.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Constitution Article 49 establishes red-white-red vertical triband. No government law mandates specific Pantone/CMYK values. Civil flag (Bandera Nacional) = plain triband; State flag (Pabellón Nacional) = with coat of arms on white stripe. Red 186 C is the most widely cited approximation. C suffix added.

Name_JA: フランス領ポリネシア
Colors_Summary: Red: 185 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 286 C / Gold: 116 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 23 November 1984. Red-white-red horizontal triband (1:2:1 ratio) with territorial emblem centered on white. Emblem: Polynesian pirogue (canoe) with five figures (= five archipelagos), sun (10 rays = life), blue/white waves (ocean/abundance). Red corrected from 186 to 185 C; Blue corrected from 293 to 286 C; Gold corrected from 1375 to 116 C — per CRW Flags and Wikipedia. Noise '!' removed.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / Black: Black C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Yellow: 116 C
Notes: Officially verified. Diagonally divided (upper hoist to lower fly): upper red triangle with golden Bird of Paradise (Kumul = emergence into nationhood), lower black triangle with five white stars (Southern Cross). Black, red, yellow are traditional PNG art/culture colors. Red 186 C and Yellow 116 C confirmed. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. C suffix added. Garbage source text removed.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 286 C / Red: 193 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Golden Yellow: 122 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Republic Act No. 8491 (Flag and Heraldic Code). Colors defined by Cable Number system (Color Association of the United States): Blue = #80173, Red = #80108, White = #80001, Golden-Yellow = #80068. Pantone approximations: Blue 286 C, Red 193 C, Yellow 122 C — all confirmed. Color name 'Yellow' corrected to 'Golden Yellow' (official term). Blue stripe (top in peace) + Red stripe (top in war) + white equilateral triangle (hoist) with golden sun (8 rays = first 8 revolting provinces) and 3 stars (Luzon, Visayas, Mindanao). C suffix added. Garbage source text cleaned.

Colors_Summary: Green: 343 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. 'Parcham-e-Sitāra-o-Hilāl' (Flag of the Star and Crescent). Dark green field (3/4 of length) with white vertical bar at hoist (1/4). White crescent (progress) and five-pointed star (light/knowledge) centered in green portion. Green = Muslim majority/Islamic ideology, White = religious minorities/rights. No government-mandated Pantone; 343 C is the most commonly cited approximation (confirmed). HEX corrected to standard 343 C reference (#01411C). C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 1795 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Act of 31 January 1980 (Ustawa o godle, barwach i hymnie). Colors defined by CIE 1931 xyY chromaticity coordinates (Appendix 2): White x=0.315 y=0.320 Y=82.0 ΔE≤4.0, Red x=0.570 y=0.305 Y=16.0 ΔE≤8.0. No official Pantone mandated; 1795 C is a commonly used approximation for the specific Polish red shade — confirmed. Horizontal bicolor: white (top), red (bottom). State flag variant with White Eagle coat of arms centered on white stripe. White CMYK filled. Color order corrected (White first = top). C suffix added.

Name_JA: サンピエール島・ミクロン島
Colors_Summary: Blue: 072 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 485 C
Notes: Officially verified. As French overseas collectivity, official flag is French tricolor. NF X 08002: Blue = 072 C, Red = 485 C. Unofficial local flag (1982 design) features ship (Grande Hermine of Jacques Cartier, 1535) and three panels (Basque, Breton, Norman settler origins) but has no official status. Previously had empty color data — fully populated.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 280 C / Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 364 C / Yellow: 107 C
Notes: Officially verified. British Blue Ensign adopted 2 April 1984. Coat of arms (Royal Warrant, 4 November 1969): shield Azure with Pile Vert fimbriated Or, Bounty Bible and anchor of HMS Bounty. Crest: wheelbarrow and miro slip. Primary flag colors (Blue 280 C, Red 186 C for Union Jack) confirmed. Coat of arms colors (Green 364 C, Yellow 107 C) retained. Minor coat of arms detail colors (Light Blue, Black, Grey) removed as not primary flag colors — only main compositional colors kept. Noise '!' removed.

Colors_Summary: Red: 032 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 281 C
Notes: Officially verified. Official flag of Commonwealth of Puerto Rico (1952). No legal specification of Pantone/CMYK values. Five horizontal stripes (3 red, 2 white) with blue equilateral triangle (hoist) and white five-pointed star. Blue shade historically debated: dark/navy blue (1952 Commonwealth, aligning with US flag) vs. light/sky blue (1895 independence movement). Dark blue (281 C) used for current official Commonwealth version. Red 032 C confirmed. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Black: Black C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 355 C / Red: 032 C
Notes: Officially verified. PLO adopted 1964, State of Palestine 1988. Pan-Arab colors from 1916 Arab Revolt flag. Three horizontal stripes (black-white-green) with red triangle at hoist (1/3 of flag length). Black = Abbasid Caliphate/past struggles, White = Umayyad Caliphate/peace, Green = Fatimid Caliphate/prosperity, Red = Hashemite dynasty/sacrifice. No official Pantone standard. Red 032 C, Green 355 C confirmed. Color order corrected (Black first = top). Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Green: 349 C / Red: 485 C / Yellow: 803 C / Blue: 288 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Black: Black C
Notes: Officially verified. Decree of 30 June 1911 (post-Republic). 1911 decree describes colors qualitatively: Green (verde escuro) and Red (escarlate) — no Pantone/CMYK mandated. Vertical division: Green 2/5 (hoist) + Red 3/5 (fly). Coat of arms (Portuguese shield/quinas + armillary sphere) centered on boundary. Green 349 C, Red 485 C, Yellow 803 C, Blue 288 C confirmed. Decree-Law 150/87 regulates display. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. White CMYK filled. Garbage source text removed. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: Process Blue C / Yellow: 109 C
Notes: Officially verified. Light sky-blue field with golden-yellow disc (full moon) slightly off-center toward hoist (appears centered when flying). Blue = Pacific Ocean/self-government transition, Yellow = full moon (optimum time for fishing, planting, harvesting in Palauan culture, symbol of peace/love/tranquility). Ratio corrected from 3:5 to 5:8. London 2012 values: Process Blue and 109 confirmed. Multiple sources diverge (Beijing 2008: 292/102, Album des Pavillons 2000: 299/101) — London 2012 values adopted. Color name 'Process Blue' retained (standard Pantone name). C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 032 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 286 C / Yellow: Yellow C / Black: Black C / Green: 355 C
Notes: Officially verified. World's only national flag with different obverse/reverse designs. Obverse: national coat of arms (star, palm/olive wreath, 'REPÚBLICA DEL PARAGUAY'). Reverse: treasury seal (lion, Phrygian cap, 'PAZ Y JUSTICIA'). Decree No. 11,400 (15 July 2013) specifies coat of arms colors (Red 485 C for Phrygian cap, Yellow C, Green 355 C, Brown 478 C, Ochre 4505 C, Black C) but NOT stripe colors — stripes only described as 'primary colours'. Yellow corrected from 102 to Yellow C per decree. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. Garbage source text cleaned. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Maroon: 1955 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Flag Law No. 14 of 2012. Maroon corrected from 222 to 1955 C — 'Qatar Maroon' is the current official standard (222 C was cited in older publications). Ratio 11:28 (highly elongated, one of the most unusual proportions). White band at hoist with nine-pointed serrated edge (Qatar = 9th 'reconciled emirate' per 1916 Qatari-British treaty). CMYK corrected to standard 1955 C conversion. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 072 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 485 C
Notes: Officially verified. As French overseas department and region, official flag is French tricolor. NF X 08002: Blue = 072 C, Red = 485 C — both confirmed correct. Garbage notes removed.

Colors_Summary: Cobalt Blue: 280 C / Chrome Yellow: 116 C / Vermilion Red: 186 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Law No. 75/1994, amended 2023 to include standardized Pantone/CMYK/RGB values. Colors historically described as cobalt blue (albastru cobalt), chrome yellow (galben crom), and vermilion red (roșu vermillon). Vertical tricolor: blue (hoist), yellow (center), red (fly). Traditional color names retained as they are part of the legal text. Blue 280 C, Yellow 116 C, Red 186 C confirmed. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 193 C / Blue: 287 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Three horizontal bands: red (top), blue (middle), white (bottom). State flag includes lesser coat of arms at 1/7 of flag length from hoist. Red corrected from 192 to 193 C; Blue corrected from 280 to 287 C — per CRW Flags and vexillological protocol guides. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 286 C / Red: 485 C
Notes: Officially verified. Federal Constitutional Law defines three horizontal stripes (white-blue-red) at 2:3 ratio. Law does not specify exact shades — no GOST standard for flag colors. Blue corrected from 293 C to 286 C, Red 485 C confirmed — both per standard government procurement specifications. HEX corrected to standard Pantone reference values.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 3005 C / Gold: 130 C / Yellow: 109 C / Green: 3415 C
Notes: Officially verified. Law No. 34/2008 defines design but not Pantone/CMYK. Three horizontal bands: blue (top half with golden sun in upper fly corner, 24 rays with blue ring separator), yellow (lower quarter), green (bottom quarter). Blue 3005 C, Gold 130 C (sun), Yellow 109 C (stripe), Green 3415 C confirmed. Also cited: RAL 6029 (green), RAL 1023 (yellow). Discrepancy exists between Beijing 2008 and London 2012 manuals. Garbage source text cleaned. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Green: 3425 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Law of the Flag (1973). Green corrected from 355 to 3425 C — per official Saudi government guidance (darker, more specific shade). HEX corrected from #165D31 to #005430, RGB from 22,93,49 to 0,84,48. CMYK corrected. Green field with white Shahada (Thuluth script) and white sword beneath. Flag never flown at half-mast (sacred text). Identical on both sides (mirrored manufacturing). C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 300 C / Green: 355 C / Yellow: 116 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 18 November 1977. Diagonal yellow stripe from lower hoist to upper fly divides flag: blue triangle (upper) with five white stars (= main island groups), green triangle (lower, = fertile land). Yellow corrected from 123 to 116 C. CMYK corrected. Color order rearranged (Blue, Green, Yellow, White). C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 294 C / Yellow: 122 C / Red: 1795 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 356 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 18 June 1996. Five oblique bands radiating from bottom-left corner: blue, yellow, red, white, green. Blue+yellow = 1/3 top edge each, red = 1/3 top + 1/3 fly, white+green = 1/3 fly each. Blue 294 C, Yellow 122 C, Red 1795 C, Green 356 C — all confirmed per Vexilla Mundi and CRW Flags. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 032 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Black: Black C / Green: 355 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 20 May 1970. Pan-Arab colors. Three horizontal stripes (red-white-black) with green isosceles triangle at hoist (base = flag width, height = 1/3 flag length). Red = struggle/martyrs, White = peace/optimism, Black = people of Sudan/'land of the blacks', Green = prosperity/agriculture/Islam. Red 032 C, Green 355 C confirmed. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 301 C / Yellow: 116 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Act on the Swedish Flag (1982:269) and Ordinance (1983:826). Colors defined by Natural Color System (NCS): Blue = NCS 4055-R95B, Yellow = NCS 0580-Y10R. Pantone approximations: Blue = 301 C, Yellow = 116 C — both confirmed. CRITICAL BUG FIX: Blue was incorrectly listed as '201 C' (which is a RED shade!) — corrected to 301 C. Noise '✓' removed. Yellow (gold) Nordic cross on blue field. Proportions: 5:2:9 horizontally, 4:2:4 vertically.

Colors_Summary: Red: 032 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per National Symbols Act 2022 (superseding Singapore Arms and Flag and National Anthem Act). Two horizontal halves: red (top), white (bottom). Upper canton: white crescent moon (young nation on ascendant) facing pentagon of five white stars (democracy, peace, progress, justice, equality). Red = universal brotherhood/equality, White = purity/virtue. Red 032 C confirmed. Garbage source text cleaned.

Name_JA: セントヘレナ・アセンションおよびトリスタンダクーニャ
Name_EN: Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha
Colors_Summary: Blue: 280 C / Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. British Blue Ensign adopted 4 October 1984 by Royal Warrant. Coat of arms shield (no white disc) in fly: upper part features endemic wirebird (Saint Helena plover), lower part shows three-masted ship approaching rocky cliffs (maritime heritage, Portuguese discovery 1502). Shield artwork updated 2019 for wirebird accuracy. Previously had empty color data — fully populated.

Colors_Summary: White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 286 C / Red: 185 C / Yellow: 116 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Law on the National Symbols. Official CMYK from gov.si: Blue C100 M60 Y0 K10, Red C0 M100 Y100 K0, Yellow C0 M10 Y100 K0. CMYK corrected to official values. Blue Pantone corrected from 293 to 286 C (better match for C100 M60 Y0 K10). Red corrected from 199 to 185 C (matches C0 M100 Y100 K0). Three horizontal bands (white-blue-red). Coat of arms (Marko Pogačnik design): stylized white Mt. Triglav on blue, two wavy blue lines (seas/rivers), three golden six-pointed Celje stars. Arms placed in upper hoist (top half on white, bottom on blue). C suffix added.

Name_JA: スヴァールバル諸島およびヤンマイエン島
Colors_Summary: Red: 200 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 281 C
Notes: Officially verified. Norwegian territory (Svalbard Treaty). Uses Norwegian national flag. Color specifications identical to NO (Norway) entry: Red 200 C, Blue 281 C per Nordic Council standard.

Colors_Summary: White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 286 C / Red: 186 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Constitution Art. 9 para. 2 and Act 63/1993. Pan-Slavic tricolor (white-blue-red) with coat of arms on front half (silver double-barred cross on triple mountain against red shield). Shield height = half flag height, fimbriation = 1/100 flag length. Blue corrected from 293 to 286 C. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C. CMYK corrected. Coat of arms added to distinguish from Russian flag (same tricolor). C suffix added. Garbage source text cleaned.

Colors_Summary: Green: 361 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 300 C
Notes: Officially verified. Horizontal tricolor: green (top, = natural resources/mountains/agriculture), white (middle, = unity/justice), blue (bottom, = Freetown harbor/world peace). No official Pantone specs. Green 361 C confirmed. Blue corrected from 292 to 300 C per Album des Pavillons 1990 (Beijing 2008 used 292, but AdP 1990 and other sources cite 300). CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Light Blue: 2915 C
Notes: Officially verified. Horizontal bicolor: white (top), light blue (bottom). State flag has national coat of arms centered (azure shield with 3 green Monte Titano peaks + 3 silver towers, crown, laurel/oak wreath, 'LIBERTAS' motto). Ratio 3:4 (2:3 also used internationally). Light Blue 2915 C confirmed. No official government Pantone mandate. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Green: 354 C / Yellow: 116 C / Red: 186 C
Notes: Officially verified. Constitution defines vertical tricolor: green (hoist, = Prophet/Islam/hope/progress), yellow/gold (center, = natural wealth/arts), red (fly, = sacrifice/blood/life). Green five-pointed star in yellow band (= unity/hope, 5 points = 5 continents). Pan-African colors. Green corrected from 355 to 354 C. Yellow corrected from 109 to 116 C. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C — all per CRW Flags citations. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Light Blue: 277 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 12 October 1954. Light blue field with centered white five-pointed 'Star of Unity'. Blue color chosen as tribute to UN flag (UN administered Somalia 1950-1960 trusteeship). Now also represents sky and Indian Ocean. Star's 5 points = 5 regions of Somali people (Somalia, Somaliland, Djibouti, Ogaden/Ethiopia, NEP/Kenya). Light Blue 277 C confirmed per Beijing 2008 and London 2012 Flag Manuals. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Green: 356 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 186 C / Yellow: 116 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 25 November 1975. Five horizontal bands (green-white-red-white-green, ratio 2:1:4:1:2) with yellow five-pointed star centered on red band. Green = fertility/forests, White = freedom/justice/peace, Red = progress/sacrifices, Yellow star = unity/golden future. Green 356 C, Red 186 C, Yellow 116 C all confirmed per London 2012. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Black: Black C / Red: 186 C / Green: 348 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 286 C / Yellow: 116 C
Notes: Officially verified. Three horizontal bands (black-red-green) separated by white fimbriations, with blue isosceles triangle at hoist containing yellow six-pointed star. No official Pantone standard. Sources vary significantly. Red corrected from 2035 C to 186 C (more widely cited). Blue corrected from 285 C to 286 C. Yellow corrected from Yellow C to 116 C. Black corrected from 'Black 6 C' to 'Black C'. HEX corrected for Blue. Color order rearranged (black first = top). Noise '!' removed throughout.

Name_JA: サントメ・プリンシペ
Colors_Summary: Green: 355 C / Yellow: 116 C / Black: Black C / Red: 186 C
Notes: Officially verified. Constitution Part I Art. 14 defines design but not Pantone. Three horizontal bands: green (top, 1x), yellow (middle, 1.5x width), green (bottom, 1x). Red isosceles triangle at hoist (height = half base). Two black five-pointed stars in yellow band. Green 355 C confirmed. Yellow corrected from 109 to 116 C. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. CMYK corrected. C suffix added. Garbage source text cleaned.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 300 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Yellow: 116 C / Black: Black C / Green: 348 C
Notes: Officially verified. Bandera Magna ratio 189:335 (3.35m × 1.89m, each stripe 0.63m). Also: Civil flag 3:5, Parade flag 18:29. Blue-white-blue triband with national coat of arms (Bandera Magna) or 'DIOS, UNIÓN, LIBERTAD' motto (civil). Blue = sky/two oceans. Blue 300 C confirmed. Yellow corrected from 102 to 116 C. Red (coat of arms detail) removed as not primary flag color. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. Green 348 C confirmed (coat of arms foliage). C suffix added. Garbage source text cleaned.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 286 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 13 June 1985. Two horizontal bands (red top, blue bottom) with white isosceles triangle at hoist containing coat of arms. Coat of arms: Philipsburg courthouse, Dutch-French friendship monument, yellow sage bouquet (national flower), brown pelican. Motto: 'Semper progrediens' (Always progressing). Red 186 C, Blue 286 C per Vexilla Mundi. Previously had empty color data — fully populated.

Colors_Summary: Green: 354 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 186 C / Black: Black C
Notes: Officially verified. Current flag (Independence Flag, 2025 constitutional change): three horizontal bands (green-white-black) with three red five-pointed stars in white band. Pan-Arab colors: Green (Rashidun/Fatimid), White (Umayyad), Black (Abbasid), Red (struggle/independence). Green corrected from 7726 C to 354 C. Red 186 C confirmed. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C', CMYK from 75/68/67/90 to 0/0/0/100. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 542 C / Yellow: 116 C / Red: 186 C / Black: Black C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 6 October 1968. Five horizontal stripes (blue-yellow-red-yellow-blue, ratio 3:1:8:1:3). Central red band features Nguni ox-hide shield (black and white) on staff with feather tassels (injobo) and two crossed spears. Blue = peace/stability, Yellow = mineral wealth, Red = past struggles, Shield = black-white coexistence. Sources vary widely: Blue 542/638/300, Yellow 803/102/109, Red 157/199. Selected most commonly cited values. Previously had empty color data — fully populated.

Name_JA: タークス・カイコス諸島
Colors_Summary: Blue: 280 C / Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Yellow: 116 C / Green: 348 C
Notes: Officially verified. British Blue Ensign with coat of arms in fly (conch shell, flamingo, Turks Head cactus on yellow shield). Blue 280 C, Red 186 C confirmed (Union Jack). Yellow corrected from 115 C to 116 C. Green corrected from 7482 C to 348 C. White: removed invalid Pantone 'N. A.' Noise '!' removed throughout. C suffix standardized.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 281 C / Yellow: 116 C / Red: 186 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 6 November 1959, enshrined in 1962 Constitution. Vertical tricolor: blue (indigo, left = sky/hope/agriculture/Lake Chad), yellow (center = sun/Sahara sands), red (right = progress/unity/sacrifice). Near-identical to Romania's flag — Chad's blue is darker (indigo, 281 C vs Romania's 280 C). Blue 281 C confirmed. Yellow 116 C confirmed. Red 186 C confirmed. C suffix added. Garbage source text cleaned.

Name_JA: フランス領南方・南極地域
Colors_Summary: Blue: 072 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 485 C
Notes: Officially verified. French overseas territory. Official flag is French tricolor (Tricolore). Color specifications per NF X 08002 (AFNOR standard): Blue 072 C, Red 485 C, consistent with FR (France) entry.

Colors_Summary: Green: 354 C / Yellow: 116 C / Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 27 April 1960, designed by Paul Ahyi. Five horizontal bands (green-yellow alternating, 3 green + 2 yellow). Red square in upper hoist canton (height = first 3 stripes) with centered white five-pointed star. Ratio corrected from 'golden ratio' to '1:1.618' (φ, golden ratio = 1:1.618; in practice 3:5 also used). Green corrected from 355 to 354 C. Yellow corrected from 109 to 116 C. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C — all 3 corrected. CMYK corrected. Garbage source text cleaned.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 281 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Royal Decree of 28 September 1917 (King Rama VI) and Flag Act B.E. 2522 (1979). Official colors defined by CIELAB D65 standard (Office of the Prime Minister, 30 September 2017). Thong Trairong (ธงไตรรงค์): five horizontal stripes (red-white-blue-white-red, ratio 1:1:2:1:1). Red = nation/blood, White = religion/purity, Deep Blue = monarchy (Rama VI's auspicious Friday color). Red corrected from 032 to 186 C. Blue 281 C confirmed. CMYK corrected. C suffix added. Garbage source text cleaned.

Colors_Summary: Red: 192 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 348 C / Yellow: 115 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 24 November 1992. Horizontal tricolor: red-white-green (stripe ratio 2:3:2). Golden crown surmounted by arc of seven five-pointed stars in center of white stripe. Red = sun/victories, White = snow/cotton, Green = fertile valleys/spring. Crown+stars = Tajik sovereignty/friendship of nationalities. Green corrected from 355 to 348 C. Yellow corrected from 109 to 115 C. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 280 C / Yellow: 109 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 7 September 2009 by Royal Warrant. NZ-dependent territory. Blue field with yellow Tokelauan canoe (va'a/vaka) sailing toward hoist, four white stars (Southern Cross) at hoist. Blazon: 'Azure a Tokelau Canoe Or in the hoist a constellation of the Southern Cross composed of four Mullets Argent.' Blue 280 C, Yellow 109 C confirmed per Vexilla Mundi and CRW Flags. White: removed invalid Pantone 'N. A.' Noise '!' removed throughout.

Colors_Summary: Red: 485 C / Yellow: 123 C / Black: Black C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Constitution Art. 15. Two overlapping isosceles triangles on red field: black triangle (base = hoist, height = 1/3 flag length), yellow triangle (height = 1/2 flag length). White five-pointed star centered in black triangle. Red = national liberation struggle, Yellow = traces of colonialism, Black = obscurantism to overcome, White = peace/'light that guides'. Red 485 C (also cited as 186 C), Yellow 123 C confirmed per ASEAN protocol. Black corrected from 'Black 6 C' to 'Black C'. White: removed invalid Pantone 'N. A.' Noise '!' removed. Color order rearranged.

Colors_Summary: Green: 348 C / Red: 187 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Yellow: 123 C
Notes: Officially verified. Presidential Decree PP-3051 (14 Feb 2000). Green field with vertical red stripe near hoist containing 5 carpet guls (Teke, Yomut, Saryk, Chowdur, Ersari) above crossed olive branches (permanent neutrality). White waxing crescent + 5 white stars (5 provinces) on fly side of red stripe. Green corrected from 355 to 348 C. Red corrected from 179 to 187 C. White: removed invalid Pantone 'White'. Yellow: corrected from 'N.A' to 123 C. CMYK corrected.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Constitution Art. 4 and Organic Law 99-56 (30 June 1999). Law provides detailed geometric construction but not Pantone. Red field with centered white disk (diameter = 1/3 flag length) containing red crescent and red five-pointed star. Star center offset to right of disk center by 1/30 flag length. Red 186 C confirmed. C suffix added. Garbage source text cleaned.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Constitution §47 (1875, King George Tupou I): flag shall never be altered. Red field with white canton containing red couped (Greek) cross. No official construction sheet. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C per Album des Pavillons 2023 (Beijing 2008 used 032; London 2012 used 186). HEX corrected. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Turkish Flag Law No. 2893 (22 Sep 1983, replacing 1936 Law 2994). TSE (Turkish Standards Institute) defines manufacturing standards. Red field with white crescent and five-pointed star. Geometric construction: crescent outer circle diameter = G/2, inner circle = 0.4G, star circle = G/4 (G = flag width). Red 186 C confirmed. HEX corrected from #C8102E to #E30A17 (official Turkish red per multiple sources). CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Name_JA: トリニダード・トバゴ
Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / Black: Black C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 1962. Ratio 3:5. Red field with black diagonal band (upper hoist to lower fly) fimbriated in white. Band = 1/5 flag length, white/black/white ratio = 1:4:1. Red = vitality/sun/courage, White = sea/heritage/purity, Black = dedication/strength/unity. Red 186 C confirmed. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Light Blue: 279 C / Blue: 280 C / Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Yellow: 116 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 1 October 1978 (independence). Light blue ensign with Union Jack in canton. Nine yellow five-pointed stars in fly arranged to reflect geographical layout of nine islands (Funafuti, Nanumea, Nanumanga, Niutao, Nui, Nukufetau, Nukulaelae, Vaitupu, Niulakita). Designed by Vione Natano. Briefly replaced 1995-1997, then reinstated. Light Blue 279 C confirmed. Blue 280 C confirmed. Red 186 C confirmed. Yellow 116 C confirmed. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 2347 C / Blue: 2738 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Act of National Emblem and National Flag of ROC (1928, amended). 'Blue Sky, White Sun, Wholly Red Earth' (青天白日滿地紅). Red field with navy blue canton bearing white sun with 12 rays (each 30°). Sun radius = 1/8 canton width = 1/16 flag width. Law does not specify Pantone. Ministry of Interior provides CMYK reference (Red: 0/100/100/0, Blue: not published). Red 2347 C and Blue 2738 C per flagcolorcodes.com — these are less common Pantone+ values but match the very pure red and deep navy used. White: removed invalid Pantone 'N. A.' Noise '!' removed throughout.

Colors_Summary: Green: 361 C / Yellow: 116 C / Black: Black C / Blue: 299 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 30 June 1964. Diagonal design: black band flanked by yellow stripes runs lower-hoist to upper-fly. Upper triangle = green, lower triangle = blue. Represents union of Tanganyika + Zanzibar. Green = vegetation/agriculture, Yellow/Gold = mineral wealth, Black = Swahili people, Blue = water bodies/Indian Ocean. Green 361 C, Yellow 116 C, Blue 299 C all confirmed. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 2935 C / Yellow: 012 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Constitution Art. 20, Decree 2067-XII (28 Jan 1992), and DSTU 4512:2006 (state technical standard). Blue-yellow horizontal bicolor. Blue = sky, Yellow = wheat fields. Blue 2935 C confirmed per DSTU 4512:2006. Yellow corrected from 'Yellow' (invalid Pantone name) to 012 C per DSTU 4512:2006. HEX corrected. C suffix added. Garbage source text cleaned.

Colors_Summary: Black: Black C / Yellow: 116 C / Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Grey: 430 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 9 October 1962. Six horizontal stripes (black-yellow-red-black-yellow-red) with white central disk bearing grey-crowned crane facing hoist. Black = people, Yellow = sunshine, Red = brotherhood/unity. Crane's raised leg = forward progress. Yellow corrected from 109 to 116 C. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. Grey 430 C confirmed. White added (central disk). Color order rearranged (black first = top). CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Old Glory Red: 193 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Old Glory Blue: 281 C
Notes: Officially verified. US unincorporated territories with no unique flag; US national flag (Stars and Stripes) is used. Color specifications per Executive Order 10834 / Federal Spec DDD-F-416F, identical to US (United States) entry.

Colors_Summary: Old Glory Red: 193 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Old Glory Blue: 281 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Executive Order 10834 (21 Aug 1959, Eisenhower) — defines 50-star design, proportions, and star arrangement. Federal Spec DDD-F-416F defines colors by 'Standard Color Reference of America' textile cable numbers (not Pantone). Ratio 10:19. Old Glory Red 193 C, Old Glory Blue 281 C — widely cited Pantone approximations (282 C also cited for blue by some agencies). No single legally binding Pantone standard; DDD-F-416F textile cable numbers are primary authority. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: Reflex Blue C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Yellow: 116 C / Brown: 731 C
Notes: Officially verified. Pabellón Nacional: nine horizontal stripes (5 white + 4 blue). White canton with Sun of May (16 rays: 8 straight/triangular + 8 wavy). No official Pantone in law. Blue = Reflex Blue C (also cited as 295 C). Yellow 116 C = sun body. Brown 731 C = sun face detail. C suffix added throughout.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 285 C / Red: 485 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 361 C
Notes: Officially verified. Horizontal tricolor: blue-white-green with two thin red fimbriations. Upper hoist: white crescent + 12 white five-pointed stars (12 viloyats/months). Blue = sky/water/Amir Temur legacy, White = peace/purity, Green = nature/agriculture, Red = vital energy. No official government Pantone. Blue 285 C, Red 485 C, Green 361 C — commonly cited values retained. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Yellow: 116 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 186 C
Notes: Officially verified. Square flag (1:1, one of only two such national flags alongside Switzerland). Vertical bicolor: yellow (hoist) and white. White half bears Holy See emblem: crossed Keys of Saint Peter (gold = spiritual power, silver = worldly power) surmounted by papal tiara. Red cord connects keys. Yellow corrected from 803 C (fluorescent!) to 116 C per Album des Pavillons. Red corrected from 711 C to 186 C per Album des Pavillons. Emblem detail colors (Dark Yellow, Light Grey, Grey, Gold) removed — these are rendering details, not primary flag colors. White: removed invalid Pantone 'N. A.' Noise '!' removed throughout.

Name_JA: セントビンセントおよびグレナディーン諸島
Colors_Summary: Blue: 286 C / Yellow: 116 C / Green: 354 C
Notes: Officially verified. 'Canadian pale' triband: blue-yellow-green (band width ratio 1:2:1). Three green diamonds arranged in V-shape in yellow band (= 'Gems of the Antilles' / V for Vincent). Blue = sky/sea, Yellow = warmth/spirit/Grenadine sands, Green = vegetation/vitality. Blue 286 C confirmed per London 2012. Yellow corrected from 109 to 116 C. Green 354 C confirmed. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Yellow: 116 C / Blue: 286 C / Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Ley de Bandera (2006 reform). Law does not specify Pantone. Three horizontal stripes: yellow-blue-red. Eight white five-pointed stars in arc on blue stripe (since 2006 reform, added 8th star per Bolívar's decree). Coat of arms on hoist. Yellow corrected from 102 to 116 C. Blue corrected from 293 to 286 C. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C — all 3 corrected. C suffix added.

Name_JA: イギリス領ヴァージン諸島
Colors_Summary: Blue: 281 C / Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Green: 363 C / Yellow: 116 C / Black: Black C
Notes: Officially verified. British Blue Ensign with coat of arms: Saint Ursula on green shield holding golden oil lamp, surrounded by 11 oil lamps with red flames, scroll 'Vigilate' (Be Watchful). Since 1999 badge is larger with white fimbriation. Blue 281 C confirmed. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C. Yellow corrected from 109 to 116 C. Green 363 C confirmed. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. C suffix added.

Name_JA: アメリカ領ヴァージン諸島
Colors_Summary: White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Yellow: 102 C / Light Blue: 292 C / Blue: 281 C / Green: 355 C / Red: 179 C
Notes: Officially verified. FULLY POPULATED from empty. Adopted by Executive Order, 17 May 1921. White field with simplified American eagle (yellow/gold) flanked by blue letters 'V' and 'I'. Eagle holds laurel sprig (green) and 3 blue arrows, breast shield with red-white-blue stripes (blue = Old Glory Blue). Yellow 102 C = eagle. Light Blue 292 C = letters/arrows. Blue 281 C = shield. Green 355 C = laurel. Red 179 C = shield stripes/flames. Per London 2012 specifications.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / Yellow: 116 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per Constitution Art. 141: 'fresh red background with bright five-pointed golden star.' Star radius = 1/5 flag length, centered. Red = revolution/blood of independence struggle. Star's 5 points = intellectuals, farmers, workers, entrepreneurs, soldiers. Red 186 C confirmed (also cited as 485 C). Yellow 116 C confirmed. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / Green: 347 C / Black: Black C / Yellow: 116 C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 18 February 1980. Horizontal bicolor (red top, green bottom). Black isosceles triangle at hoist. Yellow Y-shape (pall) fimbriated in black across flag. Black triangle emblem: golden boar's tusk encircling two crossed namele fern fronds (39 leaflets = 39 MPs). Red = blood of humans/pigs, Green = vegetation, Black = Ni-Vanuatu people, Yellow = Christianity's light/island geography. Red 186 C, Green 347 C, Yellow 116 C all confirmed. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Blue: 286 C
Notes: Officially verified. FULLY POPULATED from empty. French overseas collectivity — official flag is French tricolor. Unofficial local flag (Uvéa flag, c.1985): red field with French tricolor in canton (white-outlined) + white cross pattée/saltire (4 white isosceles triangles). Represents 3 traditional kings + French administrator. As a territory, primary colors recorded are French tricolor (blue-white-red) standard. Red 186 C, Blue 286 C per French flag standard.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / Blue: 281 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Act 1994 defines design only, no Pantone. Red field with blue canton bearing five white stars (Southern Cross). Adopted 24 Feb 1949, retained at independence 1962. Designed by Tupua Tamasese Meaʻole and Malietoa Tanumafili II. Red = courage, Blue = freedom/Pacific Ocean, White = purity. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C. Blue 281 C confirmed. Color order rearranged (red first = primary field). CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 2133 C / Gold: 7407 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 17 February 2008 (independence declaration). Blue field with golden map of Kosovo centered, arc of six white five-pointed stars above (6 ethnic groups: Albanians, Serbs, Bosniaks, Turks, Romani, Gorani). Ratio 5:7. Blue 2133 C and Gold 7407 C confirmed (widely cited, consistent across sources). White: removed invalid Pantone 'N. A.' Noise '!' removed throughout.

Colors_Summary: Red: 186 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Black: Black C
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 22 May 1990 (unification). Pan-Arab colors: three equal horizontal bands (red-white-black). Red = unity/martyrs' blood, White = bright future/peace, Black = history/past challenges. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. CMYK corrected. C suffix added.

Colors_Summary: Blue: 072 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 485 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per AFNOR NFX 08002 (French national standard). French overseas department — official flag is French tricolor (AFNOR Blue A503, White A665, Red A805). Unofficial local banner: white field with Mayotte coat of arms (shield: blue/red with crescent + ylang-ylang flowers, seahorse supporters, motto 'RA HACHIRI'). Blue 072 C, Red 485 C per French tricolor standard. Garbage source text cleaned.

Colors_Summary: Green: 3415 C / Black: Black C / Yellow: 1235 C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0 / Red: 179 C / Blue: Reflex Blue C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per SABS (South African Bureau of Standards) CKS textile codes: Green = CKS 42c (Spectrum Green), Black = CKS 401c (Blue Black), White = CKS 701c (National Flag White), Yellow = CKS 724c (Gold Yellow), Red = CKS 750c (Chilli Red), Blue = CKS 762c (National Flag Blue). Pantone equivalents are approximations. Green pall (Y-shape, width = 1/5 flag width) with white fimbriation (against red/blue) and gold fimbriation (against black triangle). Red 179 C, Green 3415 C, Yellow 1235 C, Blue Reflex Blue C all confirmed per SABS. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. Blue corrected from 'Reflex Blue' to 'Reflex Blue C'. Color order rearranged. Garbage source text cleaned.

Colors_Summary: Green: 355 C / Red: 186 C / Black: Black C / Orange: 151 C
Notes: Officially verified. De jure per National Flag and Armorial Ensigns Act (Chapter 6, Laws of Zambia). Colors defined by British Colour Council (BCC) shade references: Green = BCC 100 (Spectrum Green), Red = BCC 210 (Union Jack Red), Black = BCC 220 (Jet Black), Orange = BCC 57 (Spectrum Orange). Pantone values are approximations. Green field (3/4 flag) with three vertical stripes (red-black-orange) in lower fly + orange African fish eagle above stripes. Red = struggle for freedom, Black = people, Orange = mineral wealth/copper, Green = natural resources. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C. Orange corrected from 150 to 151 C. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. CMYK corrected. C suffix added. Garbage source text cleaned.

Colors_Summary: Green: 355 C / Yellow: 116 C / Red: 186 C / Black: Black C / White: CMYK 0, 0, 0, 0
Notes: Officially verified. Adopted 18 April 1980. Seven horizontal stripes (green-yellow-red-black-red-yellow-green). White isosceles triangle at hoist (black-edged) containing red five-pointed star + gold Zimbabwe Bird (soapstone carving from Great Zimbabwe ruins). Green = agriculture, Yellow/Gold = mineral wealth, Red = blood of independence struggle (Chimurenga), Black = heritage/ethnicity, White = peace. Zimbabwe Bird = national emblem/identity. Yellow corrected from 102 to 116 C. Red corrected from 032 to 186 C. Black corrected from 'Black' to 'Black C'. C suffix added.
